title: Weighted spatial tessellations in constrained domains
text: Spatial allocation around point locations recurring requirement ecology social–ecological research, including delineation settlement influence zones, species territories, management areas. Voronoi tessellations provide simple transparent framework dividing space among generators, standard implementations assume uniform influence unconstrained Euclidean distance. weightedVoronoi package extends framework allowing () generator-specific weights, (ii) tessellations constrained arbitrary polygonal domains using either Euclidean geodesic distance, (iii) optional resistance- terrain-informed geodesic allocation, (iv) uncertainty-aware temporal tessellation workflows.

title: Weighted spatial tessellations in constrained domains
text: weightedVoronoi provides multiple tessellation methods differ distance defined, movement constrained, analyses structured. appropriate choice depends data research question.

title: Weighted spatial tessellations in constrained domains
text: first decision distance measured. Euclidean tessellation (distance = "euclidean") Uses straight-line distance Fast simple Appropriate space open unconstrained Geodesic tessellation (distance = "geodesic") Uses shortest-path distance within domain Respects boundaries, concavities, barriers Recommended movement occur across gaps (e.g. coastlines, habitat patches)

title: Weighted spatial tessellations in constrained domains
text: Geodesic distance can modified environmental resistance: resistance (default) → movement cost uniform Custom resistance surface (resistance_rast) → incorporate land cover, risk, infrastructure, etc. Elevation-based resistance (dem_rast, use_tobler = TRUE) → movement cost increases slope Use resistance : movement equally easy everywhere terrain land cover affects accessibility

title: Weighted spatial tessellations in constrained domains
text: default, resistance isotropic: - cost depends slope magnitude directional effects (e.g. uphill vs downhill): - set anisotropy = "terrain" enables: - asymmetric movement costs - realistic modelling steep terrain

title: Weighted spatial tessellations in constrained domains
text: first define concave, irregular spatial domain set generator points heterogeneous weights.
code: crs_use <- 32636  # projected CRS (metres)  make_irregular_boundary <- function(crs = crs_use) {   # A simple concave polygon (fast, deterministic, vignette-safe)   coords <- rbind(     c(0, 0),     c(1200, 0),     c(1200, 900),     c(800, 900),     c(800, 500),     c(400, 500),     c(400, 900),     c(0, 900),     c(0, 0)   )   st_sf(geometry = st_sfc(st_polygon(list(coords))), crs = crs) }  boundary_sf <- make_irregular_boundary()  set.seed(42) pts <- st_sample(boundary_sf, size = 8, type = "random")  points_sf <- st_sf(   village = paste0("V", seq_along(pts)),   population = round(exp(rnorm(length(pts), log(300), 0.8))),   geometry = pts,   crs = crs_use )  plot(st_geometry(boundary_sf), border = "black", lwd = 2) plot(st_geometry(points_sf), add = TRUE, pch = 21, bg = "red")

title: Weighted spatial tessellations in constrained domains
text: first generate weighted tessellation using Euclidean distance. Generator influence scaled using logarithmic transformation population weights.
code: out_euc <- weighted_voronoi_domain(   points_sf = points_sf,   weight_col = "population",   boundary_sf = boundary_sf,   res = 20,   weight_transform = log10,   distance = "euclidean",   # Optional alternative weight semantics:   weight_model = "multiplicative",   clip_to_boundary = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE )  plot(st_geometry(boundary_sf), border = "black", lwd = 2) plot(out_euc$polygons["generator_id"], add = TRUE) plot(st_geometry(points_sf), add = TRUE, pch = 21, bg = "red")

title: Weighted spatial tessellations in constrained domains
text: Geodesic tessellations compute shortest-path distances constrained domain. default, geodesic allocation uses "classic" engine, computes one accumulated-cost surface per generator. additive isotropic geodesic tessellations, faster "multisource" engine also available: multisource engine currently available additive isotropic geodesic tessellations (weight_model = "additive" anisotropy = "none").
code: out_geo <- weighted_voronoi_domain(   points_sf = points_sf,   weight_col = "population",   boundary_sf = boundary_sf,   res = 20,   weight_transform = log10,   distance = "geodesic",   close_mask = TRUE,   close_iters = 1,   verbose = FALSE )  plot(st_geometry(boundary_sf), border = "black", lwd = 2) plot(out_geo$polygons["generator_id"], add = TRUE) plot(st_geometry(points_sf), add = TRUE, pch = 21, bg = "red") out_geo_fast <- weighted_voronoi_domain(   points_sf = points_sf,   weight_col = "population",   boundary_sf = boundary_sf,   res = 20,   distance = "geodesic",   weight_model = "additive",   geodesic_engine = "multisource",   verbose = FALSE )

title: Weighted spatial tessellations in constrained domains
text: many ecological social–ecological applications, effective distance shaped domain geometry also environmental resistance. Topography, land cover, infrastructure can increase block movement. recommended workflow build resistance surface (optionally composed multiple rasters), apply barriers, pass result weighted_voronoi_domain() via resistance_rast.
code: # Build a template grid (aligned to the analysis) template <- out_euc$allocation  # Two simple resistance layers (for demonstration) R_land <- template; terra::values(R_land) <- 1 R_land[template == 1] <- 2  # small spatial pattern  R_risk <- template; terra::values(R_risk) <- 1 xy <- terra::xyFromCell(R_risk, 1:terra::ncell(R_risk)) band <- xy[,1] > 450 & xy[,1] < 550 vals <- terra::values(R_risk); vals[band] <- 10; terra::values(R_risk) <- vals  # Compose layers (multiplicative by default) R <- compose_resistance(R_land, R_risk, template = template, method = "multiply")  # Add a river barrier (semi-permeable) river <- st_sf(   geometry = st_sfc(st_linestring(rbind(c(500, 0), c(500, 900)))),   crs = st_crs(boundary_sf) )  R2 <- add_barriers(R, river, permeability = "semi", cost_multiplier = 20, width = 20)  out_geo_res <- weighted_voronoi_domain(   points_sf = points_sf,   weight_col = "population",   boundary_sf = boundary_sf,   res = 20,   weight_transform = log10,   distance = "geodesic",   resistance_rast = R2,   verbose = FALSE )  plot(st_geometry(boundary_sf), border = "black", lwd = 2) plot(out_geo_res$polygons["generator_id"], add = TRUE) plot(st_geometry(points_sf), add = TRUE, pch = 21, bg = "red")

what: Geodesic tessellations with isotropic elevation-dependent resistance
title: Weighted spatial tessellations in constrained domains
text: many ecological social–ecological applications, effective distance shaped domain geometry also environmental resistance. Topography, land cover, infrastructure may increase cost movement locations. accommodate , weightedVoronoi allows geodesic distance calculations modified isotropic resistance surface. digital elevation model (DEM) supplied use_tobler = TRUE, movement cost adjusted using Tobler’s hiking function, increases effective distance across steep slopes.
code: # Create a synthetic DEM on the same grid for a lightweight vignette example bnd_v <- terra::vect(boundary_sf) dem <- terra::rast(ext = terra::ext(bnd_v), resolution = 20, crs = terra::crs(bnd_v))  xy <- terra::crds(dem, df = TRUE) y0 <- (min(xy$y) + max(xy$y)) / 2 sigma <- (max(xy$y) - min(xy$y)) / 10 height <- 800 terra::values(dem) <- height * exp(-((xy$y - y0)^2) / (2 * sigma^2))  out_geo_dem <- weighted_voronoi_domain(   points_sf = points_sf,   weight_col = "population",   boundary_sf = boundary_sf,   res = 20,   weight_transform = log10,   distance = "geodesic",   dem_rast = dem,   use_tobler = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE )  plot(st_geometry(boundary_sf), border = "black", lwd = 2) plot(out_geo_dem$polygons["generator_id"], main = "Geodesic + Tobler resistance", add = TRUE) plot(st_geometry(points_sf), add = TRUE, pch = 21, bg = "red")

title: Weighted spatial tessellations in constrained domains
text: DEM-based geodesic workflow uses isotropic resistance: movement cost depends slope magnitude, movement direction. applications, however, uphill downhill movement differ. example, steep uphill travel may costly downhill travel, even across terrain. weightedVoronoi therefore also supports terrain-anisotropic geodesic mode, movement neighbouring raster cells becomes direction-dependent. requires DEM uses directed transition graph internally. , use simple synthetic eastward-rising DEM compare isotropic terrain-anisotropic geodesic allocation.  example, uphill downhill movement treated differently, terrain-anisotropic tessellation can diverge isotropic DEM-based result. parameters uphill_factor downhill_factor control strength directional asymmetry.
code: # Terrain-anisotropy example (self-contained) crs_use2 <- 32636  boundary_sf2 <- st_sf(   id = 1,   geometry = st_sfc(     st_polygon(list(rbind(       c(0, 0),       c(1200, 0),       c(1200, 900),       c(800, 900),       c(800, 500),       c(400, 500),       c(400, 900),       c(0, 900),       c(0, 0)     ))),     crs = crs_use2   ) )  dem_aniso <- terra::rast(   ext = terra::ext(terra::vect(boundary_sf2)),   resolution = 20,   crs = terra::crs(terra::vect(boundary_sf2)) )  xy2 <- terra::crds(dem_aniso, df = TRUE) terra::values(dem_aniso) <- xy2$x * 10  pts2 <- st_sf(   village = c("A", "B"),   population = c(1, 1),   geometry = st_sfc(     st_point(c(200, 450)),     st_point(c(950, 450))   ),   crs = crs_use2 )  out_geo_iso2 <- weighted_voronoi_domain(   points_sf = pts2,   weight_col = "population",   boundary_sf = boundary_sf2,   res = 20,   distance = "geodesic",   dem_rast = dem_aniso,   use_tobler = TRUE,   anisotropy = "none",   verbose = FALSE ) #> Warning: attribute variables are assumed to be spatially constant throughout #> all geometries  out_geo_aniso2 <- weighted_voronoi_domain(   points_sf = pts2,   weight_col = "population",   boundary_sf = boundary_sf2,   res = 20,   distance = "geodesic",   dem_rast = dem_aniso,   use_tobler = TRUE,   anisotropy = "terrain",   uphill_factor = 3,   downhill_factor = 1.2,   verbose = FALSE ) #> Warning: attribute variables are assumed to be spatially constant throughout #> all geometries  par(mfrow = c(1, 2)) plot(st_geometry(boundary_sf2), border = "black", lwd = 2, main = "Isotropic DEM geodesic") plot(out_geo_iso2$polygons["generator_id"], add = TRUE) plot(st_geometry(pts2), add = TRUE, pch = 21, bg = "red")  plot(st_geometry(boundary_sf2), border = "black", lwd = 2, main = "Terrain-anisotropic geodesic") plot(out_geo_aniso2$polygons["generator_id"], add = TRUE) plot(st_geometry(pts2), add = TRUE, pch = 21, bg = "red") par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

title: Weighted spatial tessellations in constrained domains
text: applications, generator weights known exactly. explore uncertainty affects allocation, weightedVoronoi can repeat tessellation stochastic perturbation generator weights summarise results per-cell membership probabilities entropy. current implementation, uncertainty applied generator weights . simulation, weights perturbed using lognormal multiplicative model, tessellation recomputed, allocation rasters combined probability surfaces. Entropy used summarise spatial uncertainty: low entropy indicates stable assignment, whereas high entropy indicates cells switch often among generators.
code: crs_u <- "EPSG:32636"  boundary_u <- st_sf(   geometry = st_sfc(st_polygon(list(rbind(     c(0, 0),     c(200, 0),     c(200, 200),     c(0, 200),     c(0, 0)   )))),   crs = crs_u )  points_u <- st_sf(   population = c(0.01, 0.02),   geometry = st_sfc(     st_point(c(60, 100)),     st_point(c(140, 100))   ),   crs = crs_u )  out_u <- weighted_voronoi_uncertainty(   points_sf = points_u,   weight_col = "population",   boundary_sf = boundary_u,   n_sim = 100,   weight_sd = 0.8,   distance = "geodesic",   geodesic_engine = "multisource",   weight_model = "additive",   verbose = FALSE,   seed = 1 )  plot(out_u$entropy, main = "Entropy") plot(terra::vect(points_u), add = TRUE, pch = 21, col = "black", bg = "red")

title: Weighted spatial tessellations in constrained domains
text: weightedVoronoi can also run tessellations across sequence time-specific point datasets. makes possible compare allocation time allowing generator weights, locations, resistance inputs vary time step.  example, change_map_first_last identifies cells whose allocation differs first last time step, persistence highlights cells retain allocation across full temporal sequence.
code: pts_t1 <- points_u pts_t2 <- points_u pts_t2$population <- c(0.02, 0.01)  out_time <- weighted_voronoi_time(   points_list = list(time1 = pts_t1, time2 = pts_t2),   weight_col = "population",   boundary_sf = boundary_u,   distance = "geodesic",   geodesic_engine = "multisource",   weight_model = "additive",   verbose = FALSE ) #> Warning: attribute variables are assumed to be spatially constant throughout #> all geometries #> Warning: attribute variables are assumed to be spatially constant throughout #> all geometries  par(mfrow = c(1, 2)) plot(out_time$change_map_first_last, main = "Change map") plot(out_time$persistence, main = "Persistence") par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

title: Weighted spatial tessellations in constrained domains
text: Although tessellations fully partition domain, differ influence propagates concave boundaries narrow corridors. Euclidean tessellation allocates regions based straight-line distance within rasterised domain, whereas geodesic tessellation respects domain geometry prevents influence across inaccessible areas. differences reflected visually generator-level summary tables returned software.
code: out_euc$summary out_geo$summary

title: Weighted spatial tessellations in constrained domains
text: Resolution: Smaller raster cell sizes improve boundary fidelity increase computation time. Weights: Alternative weight transformations (e.g. square-root power-law) can supplied reflect different assumptions generator influence. Distance metric: Geodesic distance recommended domain geometry strongly constrains access interaction. Terrain anisotropy: Use anisotropy = "terrain" dem_rast uphill downhill movement differ. Uncertainty: entropy zero everywhere, tessellation may simply robust perturbation level used; stronger perturbations smaller spatial domain may needed reveal uncertainty. Temporal analysis: weighted_voronoi_time() supports time-specific point datasets can return change persistence maps.

title: weightedVoronoi
text: Weighted Euclidean geodesic tessellations inside arbitrary polygon domains Flexible weight semantics via weight_model weight_power Custom resistance surfaces barriers via compose_resistance() add_barriers() Terrain-informed geodesic allocation via DEM/Tobler resistance Terrain-anisotropic geodesic tessellations Scalable multisource geodesic allocation additive isotropic geodesics Uncertainty-aware tessellations probability entropy outputs Temporal tessellation stacks change persistence maps

title: weightedVoronoi
text: weightedVoronoi supports several spatial tessellation approaches depending assumptions distance, landscape structure, analysis goals.

title: weightedVoronoi
text: Straight-line influence (fastest) → distance = "euclidean" Constrained domain geometry (crossing gaps/barriers) → distance = "geodesic" Landscape affects movement (e.g. terrain, land cover) → provide resistance_rast dem_rast Uphill vs downhill matters (directional movement) → anisotropy = "terrain" Repeated runs (uncertainty time series) → use prepare_geodesic_context() + geodesic_engine = "multisource" Uncertain weights → weighted_voronoi_uncertainty() Time series tessellations → weighted_voronoi_time() detailed guide, see vignette.

title: weightedVoronoi
text: avoids rebuilding geodesic graph can substantially speed repeated runs (e.g. temporal uncertainty workflows).
code: ctx <- prepare_geodesic_context(   boundary_sf = boundary_sf,   res = 20,   geodesic_engine = "multisource" )  # Now reuse for many runs (fast) out1 <- weighted_voronoi_geodesic(   points_sf = points_t1,   weight_col = "population",   prepared = ctx )  out2 <- weighted_voronoi_geodesic(   points_sf = points_t2,   weight_col = "population",   prepared = ctx )

title: weightedVoronoi
text: Build resistance surface grid, optionally combine layers, apply barriers running geodesic tessellation.
code: # Use the Euclidean allocation grid as a convenient template template <- out_euc$allocation  # Base resistance (all 1) R <- template terra::values(R) <- 1  # Add a high-friction vertical band (e.g., dense vegetation) xy <- terra::xyFromCell(R, 1:terra::ncell(R)) band <- xy[,1] > 450 & xy[,1] < 550 vals <- terra::values(R) vals[band] <- 25 terra::values(R) <- vals  # Add a semi-permeable river barrier (vector line) river <- st_sf(   geometry = st_sfc(st_linestring(rbind(c(500, 0), c(500, 1000)))),   crs = st_crs(boundary_sf) )  # Tip: for coarse rasters, use width ~ res/2 or res R2 <- add_barriers(R, river, permeability = "semi", cost_multiplier = 20, width = 20)  out_geo_res <- weighted_voronoi_domain(   points_sf = points_sf,   weight_col = "population",   boundary_sf = boundary_sf,   res = 20,   weight_transform = log10,   distance = "geodesic",   resistance_rast = R2,   verbose = FALSE )

title: weightedVoronoi
text: Environmental resistance terrain can strongly influence spatial allocation. example shows terrain anisotropy (direction-dependent movement cost) can substantially alter geodesic tessellations compared isotropic resistance. Unlike isotropic resistance, terrain-anisotropic geodesic tessellations allow uphill downhill movement differ, producing direction-dependent allocation patterns.

text: weighted_voronoi_domain() returns: polygons: sf object one polygon per generator (attributes) allocation: terra::SpatRaster assigning raster cell generator summary: generator-level summary table (area, share, weights, etc.) diagnostics: diagnostics settings (coverage, unreachable fraction geodesic, etc.)

title: Summary
text: Many ecological social–ecological analyses require dividing space zones influence around point locations settlements, sampling sites, nests, service centres. common mathematical tool purpose Voronoi tessellation [@okabe2000spatial], partitions space every location assigned nearest generator point. However, standard Voronoi tessellations assume generators equal importance distance measured straight lines across unconstrained space. assumptions often unrealistic ecological systems sites differ size influence coastlines, mountains, lakes, fragmented habitats constrain movement access. weightedVoronoi R package generates multiplicatively weighted tessellations within arbitrary polygonal domains either Euclidean domain-constrained geodesic distance metrics. Geodesic distances may optionally incorporate resistance surfaces, enabling terrain-aware spatial allocation. package produces complete, connected tessellations conform irregular boundaries returns diagnostic outputs support reproducible ecological spatial analysis.

title: Summary
text: Ecological spatial allocation problems commonly involve assigning land cover, environmental variables, management responsibilities settlements, communities, sampling sites. contexts, allocation must often account : Heterogeneous generator importance (e.g., population size, sampling effort, service capacity), Irregular concave spatial domains (e.g., protected areas, coastlines, fragmented habitats), Non-Euclidean accessibility shaped barriers terrain. Straight-line distance may misrepresent ecological interaction accessibility mountainous regions, island systems, landscapes fragmented unsuitable habitat. researchers can compute least-cost constrained distances, translating complete spatial partitions typically requires bespoke workflows. weightedVoronoi developed provide unified reproducible solution weighted spatial allocation Euclidean domain-constrained geodesic distance assumptions. target audience includes spatial ecologists, landscape ecologists, conservation planners, researchers linking spatial covariates socially biologically defined influence zones.

title: Summary
text: Unweighted Voronoi tessellations available R packages deldir [@turner2019deldir], planar tessellation utilities included spatial point-pattern frameworks spatstat [@baddeley2015spatstat]. tools generally assume Euclidean distance integrate multiplicative generator weighting within arbitrary polygonal domains. Grid-based shortest-path least-cost distances can computed using gdistance [@vanetten2017gdistance], functionality packaged complete tessellation framework automatically produce spatial partitions enforce connectivity constraints. practice, researchers often combine multiple tools rely proprietary geographic information system software implement weighted constrained tessellations. weightedVoronoi differs integrating multiplicative weighting, arbitrary polygonal domain constraints, optional resistance-modified geodesic distance, automated enforcement single connected regions per generator, structured diagnostics within single open-source R workflow. integration provides functionality simultaneously available existing R spatial packages.

title: Summary
text: package implemented R (version 1.0.0) builds open-source spatial infrastructure including sf vector data handling [@pebesma2018sf], terra raster operations [@hijmans2025terra], optionally gdistance graph-based shortest-path computation grids [@vanetten2017gdistance]. raster-based architecture chosen allow tessellations within complex, concave, fragmented domains enable geodesic resistance-aware distance calculations. analytic Voronoi constructions efficient unconstrained planar settings, less flexible arbitrary polygonal masks non-Euclidean distances [@okabe2000spatial]. raster approach provides geometric generality cost resolution-dependent computation time. core function, weighted_voronoi_domain(), accepts generator points weights, polygonal boundary, raster resolution, weight transformation function, distance mode. Allocation follows multiplicative rule raster cell assigned generator minimizing weighted distance. Post-processing enforces single connected regions per generator fills unreachable cells geodesic mode guarantee complete domain coverage. design prioritizes transparency, reproducibility, flexibility ecological applications boundary geometry accessibility constraints central.

title: Summary
text: package released MIT license archived versioned Zenodo DOI ensure long-term reproducibility citability. includes unit tests (testthat), continuous integration via automated R-CMD-check workflows, reproducible example vignettes support research-ready deployment initial release. deterministic allocation algorithm ensures identical outputs given identical inputs resolution, facilitating reproducible spatial analysis. integrating weighted allocation, domain constraints, geodesic distance within single documented workflow, package provides research-grade alternative ad hoc scripting proprietary geographic information system implementations.

title: Summary
code: library(sf) library(weightedVoronoi)  crs_use <- 32636  boundary_sf <- st_sf(   geometry = st_sfc(st_polygon(list(rbind(     c(0, 0), c(1200, 0), c(1200, 800), c(0, 800), c(0, 0)   )))),   crs = crs_use )  set.seed(1) pts <- st_sample(boundary_sf, size = 8)  points_sf <- st_sf(   population = round(exp(rnorm(length(pts), log(300), 0.8))),   geometry = pts,   crs = crs_use )  out <- weighted_voronoi_domain(   points_sf = points_sf,   weight_col = "population",   boundary_sf = boundary_sf,   res = 20,   weight_transform = log10,   distance = "euclidean",   verbose = FALSE )

what: Add barriers to a resistance surface — add_barriers
title: Add barriers to a resistance surface — add_barriers
text: Modifies resistance raster applying semi-permeable impermeable barriers provided raster mask (values > 0 treated barrier) vector features (sf / SpatVector) rasterised onto resistance grid.

title: Add barriers to a resistance surface — add_barriers
code: add_barriers(   resistance,   barriers,   permeability = c("semi", "impermeable", "permeable"),   cost_multiplier = 10,   width = 0 )

title: Add barriers to a resistance surface — add_barriers
text: resistance terra::SpatRaster strictly positive movement resistance. barriers terra::SpatRaster mask (values > 0 treated barrier), sf/SpatVector LINESTRING/POLYGON object. permeability One "semi", "impermeable", "permeable". cost_multiplier Numeric > 0. Multiplier applied barrier present ("semi" "permeable"). width Buffer distance (CRS units) applied vector barriers rasterising.

title: Add barriers to a resistance surface — add_barriers
text: terra::SpatRaster resistance surface barrier effects applied.

what: Compose a resistance surface from multiple raster layers — compose_resistance
title: Compose a resistance surface from multiple raster layers — compose_resistance
text: Aligns one terra::SpatRaster layers common template (CRS, resolution, extent) combines single resistance raster using specified rule. Intended building resistance_rast inputs geodesic tessellations.

title: Compose a resistance surface from multiple raster layers — compose_resistance
code: compose_resistance(   ...,   template = NULL,   mask = NULL,   method = c("multiply", "add", "max"),   resample_method = c("bilinear", "near"),   na_policy = c("propagate", "ignore") )

title: Compose a resistance surface from multiple raster layers — compose_resistance
text: ... One terra::SpatRaster layers. layers must represent strictly positive resistance values. template Optional terra::SpatRaster defining target grid (CRS, resolution, extent). Defaults first layer. mask Optional mask applied alignment. Can terra::SpatRaster, terra::SpatVector, sf polygon. method combine layers: "multiply" (default), "add", "max". resample_method Resampling method used aligning layers: "bilinear" continuous resistance, "near" categorical rasters. na_policy treat NA values: "propagate" makes NA propagate output; "ignore" drops NAs (neutral element chosen method).

title: Compose a resistance surface from multiple raster layers — compose_resistance
text: terra::SpatRaster resistance surface template grid.

title: Compose a resistance surface from multiple raster layers — compose_resistance
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ R <- compose_resistance(slope_resistance, landcover_resistance, method = "multiply") R <- add_barriers(R, rivers_sf, permeability = "semi", cost_multiplier = 20, width = 30) out <- weighted_voronoi_domain(points_sf, "w", boundary_sf, distance = "geodesic",                                resistance_rast = R) } # }

what: Prepare a geodesic context for repeated runs — prepare_geodesic_context
title: Prepare a geodesic context for repeated runs — prepare_geodesic_context
text: Precomputes domain mask, aligned resistance handling, transition object, multisource graph representation (applicable) repeated geodesic tessellation workflows.

title: Prepare a geodesic context for repeated runs — prepare_geodesic_context
code: prepare_geodesic_context(   boundary_sf,   res = 20,   close_mask = TRUE,   close_iters = 1,   resistance_rast = NULL,   dem_rast = NULL,   use_tobler = TRUE,   tobler_v0_kmh = 6,   tobler_a = 3.5,   tobler_b = 0.05,   min_speed_kmh = 0.25,   anisotropy = c("none", "terrain"),   uphill_factor = 1,   downhill_factor = 1,   geodesic_engine = c("classic", "multisource") )

title: Prepare a geodesic context for repeated runs — prepare_geodesic_context
text: boundary_sf sf POLYGON/MULTIPOLYGON defining domain. res Numeric. Raster resolution CRS units (e.g. metres). close_mask Logical. TRUE, applies morphological closing raster mask. close_iters Integer. Number closing iterations. resistance_rast Optional SpatRaster giving movement resistance (>0). dem_rast Optional SpatRaster providing elevation resistance surface. use_tobler Logical; TRUE, apply Tobler's hiking function convert slope isotropic movement cost. tobler_v0_kmh Base walking speed flat terrain (km/h). tobler_a Tobler exponential slope coefficient. tobler_b Tobler slope multiplier. min_speed_kmh Minimum allowed speed avoid infinite costs. anisotropy Character. One "none" "terrain". uphill_factor Numeric > 0. Additional uphill movement penalty anisotropy = "terrain". downhill_factor Numeric > 0. Relative ease downhill movement anisotropy = "terrain". geodesic_engine Character. One "classic" "multisource".

title: Prepare a geodesic context for repeated runs — prepare_geodesic_context
text: prepared geodesic context object repeated geodesic allocation.

title: Weighted Euclidean tessellation (core) — weighted_voronoi
text: Internal/core function used weighted_voronoi_domain() compute weighted Euclidean tessellation rasterised domain.

title: Weighted Euclidean tessellation (core) — weighted_voronoi
code: weighted_voronoi(   points_sf,   weight_col,   boundary = NULL,   template_rast = NULL,   res = NULL,   weight_transform = function(w) w,   weight_model = c("multiplicative", "power", "additive"),   weight_power = 1,   method = c("argmin", "partition"),   max_dist = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   island_min_cells = 5,   island_fill_iter = 50 )

title: Weighted Euclidean tessellation (core) — weighted_voronoi
text: points_sf sf POINT object containing generator locations attributes. weight_col Character. Name weight column points_sf. boundary Optional sf polygon defining tessellation domain. Used template_rast NULL. template_rast Optional terra::SpatRaster template raster. Provide instead boundary + res. res Numeric. Raster resolution CRS units (e.g. metres). weight_transform Function. Transforms weights allocation. Must return finite, strictly positive values. weight_model Character. One "multiplicative", "power", "additive". Controls distances weights combine effective cost. weight_power Numeric > 0. used weight_model = "power". Controls distance exponent. method Character. Allocation method; one "argmin" "partition". max_dist Optional numeric. Maximum Euclidean distance consider (euclidean ). verbose Logical. TRUE, prints progress. island_min_cells Integer. Minimum patch size used island removal. island_fill_iter Integer. Maximum iterations filling reassigned cells.

title: Weighted Euclidean tessellation (core) — weighted_voronoi
text: list containing polygon output (requested), allocation raster, weights.

what: Weighted tessellation in a constrained polygon domain — weighted_voronoi_domain
title: Weighted tessellation in a constrained polygon domain — weighted_voronoi_domain
text: Creates complete, connected tessellation polygonal domain using either weighted Euclidean distance weighted geodesic (domain-constrained) distance. Weights supplied attribute generator points can transformed user-defined function prior allocation.

title: Weighted tessellation in a constrained polygon domain — weighted_voronoi_domain
code: weighted_voronoi_domain(   points_sf,   weight_col,   boundary_sf,   res = 20,   weight_transform = function(w) w,   weight_model = c("multiplicative", "power", "additive"),   weight_power = 1,   distance = c("euclidean", "geodesic"),   max_dist = NULL,   island_min_cells = 5,   island_fill_iter = 50,   clip_to_boundary = TRUE,   close_mask = TRUE,   close_iters = 1,   resistance_rast = NULL,   dem_rast = NULL,   use_tobler = TRUE,   tobler_v0_kmh = 6,   tobler_a = 3.5,   tobler_b = 0.05,   min_speed_kmh = 0.25,   anisotropy = c("none", "terrain"),   uphill_factor = 1,   downhill_factor = 1,   geodesic_engine = c("classic", "multisource"),   prepared = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Weighted tessellation in a constrained polygon domain — weighted_voronoi_domain
text: points_sf sf POINT object containing generator locations attributes. weight_col Character. Name weight column points_sf. boundary_sf sf POLYGON/MULTIPOLYGON defining domain. res Numeric. Raster resolution CRS units (e.g. metres). weight_transform Function. Transforms weights allocation. Must return finite, strictly positive values. weight_model Character. One "multiplicative", "power", "additive". Controls distances weights combine effective cost. weight_power Numeric > 0. used weight_model = "power". Controls distance exponent. distance Character. One "euclidean" "geodesic". max_dist Optional numeric. Maximum Euclidean distance consider (euclidean ). island_min_cells Integer. Minimum patch size used island removal. island_fill_iter Integer. Maximum iterations filling reassigned cells. clip_to_boundary Logical. TRUE, polygon output intersected input boundary exact edge matching (euclidean ). close_mask Logical. TRUE, applies morphological closing raster mask (geodesic ). close_iters Integer. Number closing iterations (geodesic ). resistance_rast Optional SpatRaster giving movement resistance (>0). Overrides dem_rast/Tobler provided. dem_rast Optional SpatRaster providing elevation resistance surface. Must align tessellation domain resolution. use_tobler Logical; TRUE, apply Tobler's hiking function convert slope isotropic movement cost. tobler_v0_kmh Base walking speed flat terrain (km/h). tobler_a Tobler exponential slope coefficient (default -3.5). tobler_b Tobler slope multiplier (default 0.05). min_speed_kmh Minimum allowed speed avoid infinite costs. anisotropy Character. Directional cost model geodesic distance. "none" Standard isotropic geodesic distance (default). "terrain" Direction-dependent movement based terrain slope (DEM required). uphill_factor Numeric > 0. Multiplier controlling additional cost uphill movement anisotropy = "terrain". Values > 1 penalise uphill movement strongly. downhill_factor Numeric > 0. Multiplier controlling ease downhill movement anisotropy = "terrain". Values > 1 make downhill travel easier. geodesic_engine Character. Geodesic allocation engine use distance = "geodesic". "classic" Per-generator accumulated-cost allocation. Supports current geodesic modes weight models. "multisource" Single-pass multisource allocation. Currently supported weight_model = "additive" anisotropy = "none". prepared Optional prepared geodesic context created prepare_geodesic_context() repeated compatible geodesic runs. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints progress.

title: Weighted tessellation in a constrained polygon domain — weighted_voronoi_domain
text: list elements including: polygons sf object one polygon per generator. allocation terra::SpatRaster assigning cell generator. summary generator-level summary table. diagnostics list diagnostic metrics settings. geodesic allocation, geodesic_engine = "classic" computes one accumulated-cost surface per generator assigns raster cell minimum effective cost. reference implementation supports current geodesic modes. geodesic_engine = "multisource" provides scalable alternative additive-weight isotropic geodesic tessellations. uses single multisource shortest-path propagation currently available weight_model = "additive" anisotropy = "none".

title: Weighted tessellation in a constrained polygon domain — weighted_voronoi_domain
text: distance = "geodesic", distances computed shortest paths constrained spatial domain. dem_rast supplied use_tobler = TRUE, movement cost adjacent raster cells modified using Tobler's hiking function, steeper slopes increase effective distance. allows elevation resistance surfaces influence spatial allocation preserving complete tessellation. distance = "geodesic" anisotropy = "terrain", movement costs computed using direction-dependent extension Tobler-like hiking function. Movement raster cells becomes asymmetric: uphill downhill transitions different costs. results anisotropic (direction-dependent) geodesic tessellations. Currently, anisotropic terrain mode: requires dem_rast input combine user-supplied resistance_rast uses 8-directional neighbourhood transitions

title: Weighted tessellation in a constrained polygon domain — weighted_voronoi_domain
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(sf) crs_use <- 32636 boundary_sf <- st_sf(   geometry = st_sfc(st_polygon(list(rbind(     c(0,0), c(1000,0), c(1000,1000), c(0,1000), c(0,0)   )))),   crs = crs_use ) points_sf <- st_sf(   population = c(50, 200, 1000),   geometry = st_sfc(st_point(c(200,200)), st_point(c(800,250)), st_point(c(500,500))),   crs = crs_use ) out <- weighted_voronoi_domain(points_sf, "population", boundary_sf,   res = 20, weight_transform = log10, distance = "euclidean", verbose = FALSE ) } # } if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(sf) library(terra)  crs_use <- "EPSG:3857"  boundary_sf <- st_sf(   id = 1,   geometry = st_sfc(     st_polygon(list(rbind(       c(0, 0), c(1000, 0), c(1000, 1000),       c(0, 1000), c(0, 0)     ))),     crs = crs_use   ) )  points_sf <- st_sf(   population = c(1, 1),   geometry = st_sfc(     st_point(c(200, 500)),     st_point(c(800, 500)),     crs = crs_use   ) )  dem_rast <- rast(   ext = ext(0, 1000, 0, 1000),   resolution = 50,   crs = crs_use )  xy <- crds(dem_rast, df = TRUE) values(dem_rast) <- xy$x * 20  out <- weighted_voronoi_domain(   points_sf = points_sf,   weight_col = "population",   boundary_sf = boundary_sf,   distance = "geodesic",   dem_rast = dem_rast,   anisotropy = "terrain",   uphill_factor = 3,   downhill_factor = 1.2 ) } # }

title: Weighted geodesic tessellation (core) — weighted_voronoi_geodesic
text: Computes weighted tessellation using domain-constrained (geodesic) distances. Distances calculated shortest-path distances rasterised domain mask.

title: Weighted geodesic tessellation (core) — weighted_voronoi_geodesic
code: weighted_voronoi_geodesic(   points_sf,   weight_col,   boundary_sf,   res = 20,   weight_transform = function(w) w,   weight_model = c("multiplicative", "power", "additive"),   weight_power = 1,   close_mask = TRUE,   close_iters = 1,   resistance_rast = NULL,   dem_rast = NULL,   use_tobler = TRUE,   tobler_v0_kmh = 6,   tobler_a = 3.5,   tobler_b = 0.05,   min_speed_kmh = 0.25,   anisotropy = c("none", "terrain"),   uphill_factor = 1,   downhill_factor = 1,   island_min_cells = 5,   island_fill_iter = 50,   geodesic_engine = c("classic", "multisource"),   return_polygons = TRUE,   prepared = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Weighted geodesic tessellation (core) — weighted_voronoi_geodesic
text: points_sf sf POINT object containing generator locations attributes. weight_col Character. Name weight column points_sf. boundary_sf sf POLYGON/MULTIPOLYGON defining domain. res Numeric. Raster resolution CRS units (e.g. metres). weight_transform Function. Transforms weights allocation. Must return finite, strictly positive values. weight_model Character. One "multiplicative", "power", "additive". Controls distances weights combine effective cost. weight_power Numeric > 0. used weight_model = "power". Controls distance exponent. close_mask Logical. TRUE, applies morphological closing raster mask (geodesic ). close_iters Integer. Number closing iterations (geodesic ). resistance_rast Optional SpatRaster giving movement resistance (>0). Overrides dem_rast/Tobler provided. dem_rast Optional SpatRaster providing elevation resistance surface. Must align tessellation domain resolution. use_tobler Logical; TRUE, apply Tobler's hiking function convert slope isotropic movement cost. tobler_v0_kmh Base walking speed flat terrain (km/h). tobler_a Tobler exponential slope coefficient (default -3.5). tobler_b Tobler slope multiplier (default 0.05). min_speed_kmh Minimum allowed speed avoid infinite costs. anisotropy Character. Directional cost model geodesic distance. "none" Standard isotropic geodesic distance (default). "terrain" Direction-dependent movement based terrain slope (DEM required). uphill_factor Numeric > 0. Multiplier controlling additional cost uphill movement anisotropy = "terrain". Values > 1 penalise uphill movement strongly. downhill_factor Numeric > 0. Multiplier controlling ease downhill movement anisotropy = "terrain". Values > 1 make downhill travel easier. island_min_cells Integer. Minimum patch size used island removal. island_fill_iter Integer. Maximum iterations filling reassigned cells. geodesic_engine Character. Geodesic allocation engine; one "classic" "multisource". return_polygons Logical. TRUE, polygonise cleaned allocation raster attach point attributes. FALSE, return allocation outputs . prepared Optional prepared geodesic context created prepare_geodesic_context() repeated compatible geodesic runs. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints progress.

title: Weighted geodesic tessellation (core) — weighted_voronoi_geodesic
text: list containing polygon output, allocation raster, weights.

title: Temporal weighted tessellation — weighted_voronoi_time
text: Runs weighted tessellation across sequence time-specific point datasets returns stack allocation rasters, optional polygons summaries per time step.

title: Temporal weighted tessellation — weighted_voronoi_time
code: weighted_voronoi_time(   points_list,   weight_col,   boundary_sf,   time_index = NULL,   distance = c("euclidean", "geodesic"),   geodesic_engine = c("multisource", "classic"),   res = 20,   resistance_list = NULL,   dem_list = NULL,   keep_polygons = FALSE,   keep_summaries = TRUE,   prepared = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Temporal weighted tessellation — weighted_voronoi_time
text: points_list non-empty list sf POINT objects, one per time step. weight_col Character. Name weight column present element points_list. boundary_sf sf POLYGON/MULTIPOLYGON defining domain. time_index Optional character vector time labels. Defaults names(points_list) present, otherwise "t1", "t2", etc. distance Character. One "euclidean" "geodesic". geodesic_engine Character. Geodesic engine use distance = "geodesic". res Numeric. Raster resolution CRS units (e.g. metres). resistance_list Optional list resistance rasters, either length 1 (reused times) length points_list. dem_list Optional list DEM rasters, either length 1 (reused times) length points_list. keep_polygons Logical. TRUE, return polygons time step. keep_summaries Logical. TRUE, return summaries time step. prepared Optional prepared geodesic context created prepare_geodesic_context() repeated compatible geodesic runs. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints progress. ... Additional arguments passed weighted_voronoi_domain().

title: Temporal weighted tessellation — weighted_voronoi_time
text: list containing: allocations terra::SpatRaster one allocation layer per time step. time_index Character vector time labels. change_map_first_last terra::SpatRaster indicating whether allocation changed first last time step (1 = changed, 0 = unchanged). persistence terra::SpatRaster indicating whether cell retained allocation across time steps (1 = persistent, 0 = changed least ). polygons Optional list sf polygon outputs time. summaries Optional list summary tables time.

title: Temporal weighted tessellation — weighted_voronoi_time
text: first implementation assumes static boundary runs time step independently. Time-varying weights, point locations, resistance/DEM surfaces supported supplying separate inputs time step.

title: Uncertainty-aware weighted tessellation — weighted_voronoi_uncertainty
text: Repeats weighted tessellation stochastic perturbation generator weights summarises results per-cell membership probabilities, modal allocation, entropy.

title: Uncertainty-aware weighted tessellation — weighted_voronoi_uncertainty
code: weighted_voronoi_uncertainty(   points_sf,   weight_col,   boundary_sf,   n_sim = 100,   weight_sd = NULL,   distance = c("euclidean", "geodesic"),   geodesic_engine = c("multisource", "classic"),   res = 20,   keep_simulations = FALSE,   seed = NULL,   warn_zero_entropy = TRUE,   prepared = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Uncertainty-aware weighted tessellation — weighted_voronoi_uncertainty
text: points_sf sf POINT object containing generator locations attributes. weight_col Character. Name weight column points_sf. boundary_sf sf POLYGON/MULTIPOLYGON defining domain. n_sim Integer. Number simulation runs. weight_sd Optional numeric. Standard deviation lognormal weight perturbation log scale. NULL, perturbation applied tessellation repeated. distance Character. One "euclidean" "geodesic". geodesic_engine Character. Geodesic engine use distance = "geodesic". Defaults "multisource". res Numeric. Raster resolution CRS units (e.g. metres). keep_simulations Logical. TRUE, return simulated allocation rasters stack. seed Optional integer random seed reproducibility. warn_zero_entropy Logical. TRUE, warn entropy values zero across domain. prepared Optional prepared geodesic context created prepare_geodesic_context() repeated compatible geodesic runs. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints progress. ... Additional arguments passed weighted_voronoi_domain().

title: Uncertainty-aware weighted tessellation — weighted_voronoi_uncertainty
text: list probability surfaces, modal allocation, entropy, optionally full simulation stack.

title: Uncertainty-aware weighted tessellation — weighted_voronoi_uncertainty
text: first implementation supports uncertainty generator weights . Weights perturbed independently across simulations using lognormal multiplicative model: w_sim = w * exp(N(0, weight_sd)) output includes: probabilities terra::SpatRaster one layer per generator, containing fraction simulations cell assigned generator. modal_allocation terra::SpatRaster giving probable generator cell. entropy terra::SpatRaster showing per-cell uncertainty. Higher values indicate less stable allocation across simulations.
